title: subprocess.run text=True fails on binary stdout
summary: subprocess.run with text=True raises UnicodeDecodeError on binary output; fix is to omit text=True and decode manually with errors='replace'.
body:
## Problem

UnicodeDecodeError when calling subprocess.run with text=True on a command that outputs binary data.

## Fix

Remove `text=True`. Capture as bytes and decode with `output.decode('utf-8', errors='replace')`.

```python
result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True)
output = result.stdout.decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
```

title: Use claude-code backend to avoid API key requirement
summary: Made claude-code the default backend so users with Claude Pro can run extraction without a separate ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, using their active session credentials instead.
body:
## Decision

Default backend set to `claude-code` in knowledge.json.

## Rationale

Most users have Claude Pro but not necessarily an API key. The claude-code path reuses active session auth and requires no credential setup.

## Alternatives Considered

- `anthropic` backend: requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY setup — friction for most users.
- `bedrock` backend: requires AWS credentials — only appropriate for enterprise users.

title: Claude Code subprocess env vars must be stripped before claude -p
summary: Strip CLAUDECODE, CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT, CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_FEEDBACK_SURVEY, and CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC before spawning claude -p to prevent nested-session hangs.
body:
## Reference

When spawning `claude -p` from inside a Claude Code session, these four environment variables must be stripped from the subprocess environment:

- `CLAUDECODE` — triggers nested-session guard
- `CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT` — prevents API connections
- `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_FEEDBACK_SURVEY` — contributes to API failures
- `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC` — contributes to API failures

See: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/26190
